rationale: Ground truth is a trivial approve ('lgtm', no inline comments), so recall is vacuously satisfied by both. X's review is fully grounded — it verifies the rename against upstream and existing tests and correctly recommends approval with no fabricated claims. Y requests changes over a speculative, out-of-scope missing-test claim for a purely mechanical rename PR (contradicting the actual lgtm/approve outcome), and its output is degraded by identical triplicated review_text blocks, suggesting a process glitch rather than a clean single verdict.
